texts.orig: Solution-diffusion model
texts.text: Solution-diffusion model

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Porous membranes (pore diameter > 10Å)
texts.text: Porous membranes (pore diameter > 10Å)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The pore-flow model is applicable. The different compounds are transported by a pressure-driven convective flow through tiny membrane pores
texts.text: The pore-flow model is applicable. The different compounds are transported by a pressure-driven convective flow through tiny membrane pores

texts.label: text
texts.orig: => Separation achieved due to differences in the steric hindrances between the compounds molecules and the membrane material
texts.text: => Separation achieved due to differences in the steric hindrances between the compounds molecules and the membrane material

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Dense membranes (pore diameter < 10Å)
texts.text: Dense membranes (pore diameter < 10Å)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The solution-diffusion model is applicable. The different compounds first dissolve in the membrane matrix and then diffuse through the membrane under a concentration gradient
texts.text: The solution-diffusion model is applicable. The different compounds first dissolve in the membrane matrix and then diffuse through the membrane under a concentration gradient

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: => Separation achieved due to differences in the solubility of each compound into the membrane material and their diffusivity through the membrane
texts.text: => Separation achieved due to differences in the solubility of each compound into the membrane material and their diffusivity through the membrane

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reverse osmosis
texts.text: Reverse osmosis

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Osmosis (reverse or normal) refers to the use of a permselective membrane (membrane that is freely permeable to water but much less permeable to salt) in order to separate a salt solution from pure water. More generally, to derive the equations, we have to consider the separation of liquids .
texts.text: Osmosis (reverse or normal) refers to the use of a permselective membrane (membrane that is freely permeable to water but much less permeable to salt) in order to separate a salt solution from pure water. More generally, to derive the equations, we have to consider the separation of liquids .

texts.orig: Application: water desalination
texts.text: Application: water desalination

texts.label: text
texts.orig: At t=0s, we have side (1) that has salt water and side (2) that has only water. If both p 1 and p 2 are atmospheric pressure, then we have :
texts.text: At t=0s, we have side (1) that has salt water and side (2) that has only water. If both p 1 and p 2 are atmospheric pressure, then we have :

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The osmotic pressure Δπ is the pressure difference necessary to have a null water flux through the membrane, due to the concentration differences in the two sides of the membrane :
texts.text: The osmotic pressure Δπ is the pressure difference necessary to have a null water flux through the membrane, due to the concentration differences in the two sides of the membrane :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · p 1 - p 2 < Δπ : Normal osmosis, water flows from the pure water side to the sea-water side
texts.text: p 1 - p 2 < Δπ : Normal osmosis, water flows from the pure water side to the sea-water side

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · p 1 - p 2 = Δπ : Osmotic equilibrium, no flux since the osmotic pressure is counterbalanced by the pressure difference
texts.text: p 1 - p 2 = Δπ : Osmotic equilibrium, no flux since the osmotic pressure is counterbalanced by the pressure difference

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · p 1 - p 2 > Δπ : Reverse osmosis, water flows from the sea water side to the pure water side : water desalination
texts.text: p 1 - p 2 > Δπ : Reverse osmosis, water flows from the sea water side to the pure water side : water desalination

texts.orig: Gas permeation
texts.text: Gas permeation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Gas permeation corresponds to the separation of gases . Typically, a high pressure gas mixture at p R is fed to one side of the membrane while the permeate is removed on the other side at a lower pressure p P

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Diffusion coefficient, sorption coefficient and permeability
texts.text: Diffusion coefficient, sorption coefficient and permeability

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In polymeric membranes, D i , K i (g) and Q i typically depend on the molecular volume:
texts.text: In polymeric membranes, D i , K i (g) and Q i typically depend on the molecular volume:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Diffusion coeff. decreases with molar volume (larger molecules interact more with the polymer chain)
texts.text: Diffusion coeff. decreases with molar volume (larger molecules interact more with the polymer chain)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sorption coeff. increases with molar volume (larger molecules are usually more condensable)
texts.text: Sorption coeff. increases with molar volume (larger molecules are usually more condensable)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Permeability is proportional to the product of D i and K i . For natural rubber membranes sorption dominates, for glass membranes diffusion dominates.
texts.text: Permeability is proportional to the product of D i and K i . For natural rubber membranes sorption dominates, for glass membranes diffusion dominates.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Rubbery polymer: Q i increasing with V m
texts.text: Rubbery polymer: Q i increasing with V m

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Glassy polymer: Q i decreasing with V m
texts.text: Glassy polymer: Q i decreasing with V m

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Recovery and purity behave oppositely against the membrane area. The recovery increases while the purity decreases due to the permeation of the least permeant component along the module.
texts.text: Recovery and purity behave oppositely against the membrane area. The recovery increases while the purity decreases due to the permeation of the least permeant component along the module.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: => High permeate purity is only achievable at low recovery rate, which is not economical
texts.text: => High permeate purity is only achievable at low recovery rate, which is not economical

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · An increase in selectivity (decrease in permeability) leads to an increase in purity (decrease in recovery).
texts.text: An increase in selectivity (decrease in permeability) leads to an increase in purity (decrease in recovery).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · For large selectivity and pressure ratio, the permeate purity is almost independent from the membrane area
texts.text: For large selectivity and pressure ratio, the permeate purity is almost independent from the membrane area

texts.text: Pervaporation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Pervaporation can be considered as an intermediate separation process between gas separation and reverse osmosis processes : the retentate is in liquid phase and the permeate is in gas phase . The feed pressure is sufficient to maintain liquid state on the retentate side even as the composition is changing. The permeate pressure is fixed to a value below the saturation pressure of the permeating mixture, often applying vacuum.
texts.text: Pervaporation can be considered as an intermediate separation process between gas separation and reverse osmosis processes : the retentate is in liquid phase and the permeate is in gas phase . The feed pressure is sufficient to maintain liquid state on the retentate side even as the composition is changing. The permeate pressure is fixed to a value below the saturation pressure of the permeating mixture, often applying vacuum.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The isothermal assumption will be assumed for simplicity, but in reality an energy balance equation should be coupled with material balances to account for the heat of vaporization.
texts.text: The isothermal assumption will be assumed for simplicity, but in reality an energy balance equation should be coupled with material balances to account for the heat of vaporization.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Pervaporation: Separation Factor
texts.text: Pervaporation: Separation Factor

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We can define the vapor pressures of the liquid retentate (p i,R ) and of the permeate (p i,P ) :
texts.text: We can define the vapor pressures of the liquid retentate (p i,R ) and of the permeate (p i,P ) :

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The separation factor indicates how well was the component i separated compared to component j. To note that we have :
texts.text: The separation factor indicates how well was the component i separated compared to component j. To note that we have :

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In pervaporation, the separation factor is a product of two terms: One represents the contribution of relative volatility, and the other the contribution of the membrane.
texts.text: In pervaporation, the separation factor is a product of two terms: One represents the contribution of relative volatility, and the other the contribution of the membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Now we have three factors: the contribution of relative volatility, the selectivity of the membrane and the vapor pressures of feed and permeate (operating parameters).
texts.text: Now we have three factors: the contribution of relative volatility, the selectivity of the membrane and the vapor pressures of feed and permeate (operating parameters).

texts.orig: All these effects are dominated by the fact that α is not constant, but usually a strong function of y R :
texts.text: All these effects are dominated by the fact that α is not constant, but usually a strong function of y R :

texts.label: formula
texts.orig: α = α (y R )

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hence it is possible to have both α>>(p R /p P ) and (p R /p P )>>α for the same membrane and ratio p R /p P , simply by varying y R .
texts.text: Hence it is possible to have both α>>(p R /p P ) and (p R /p P )>>α for the same membrane and ratio p R /p P , simply by varying y R .

texts.orig: Hydrophilic membrane material:
texts.text: Hydrophilic membrane material:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · High Henry's constant for water, low Henry's constant for Ethanol
texts.text: High Henry's constant for water, low Henry's constant for Ethanol

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Strong swelling if y H2O,R >> y EtOH,R
texts.text: Strong swelling if y H2O,R >> y EtOH,R

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · High permeance and rather low selectivity if y H2O,R >> y EtOH,R
texts.text: High permeance and rather low selectivity if y H2O,R >> y EtOH,R

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · No swelling if y EtOH,R >> y H2O,R
texts.text: No swelling if y EtOH,R >> y H2O,R

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Low permeance and rather high selectivity if y EtOH,R >> y H2O,R
texts.text: Low permeance and rather high selectivity if y EtOH,R >> y H2O,R

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Design considerations for pervaporation processes
texts.text: Design considerations for pervaporation processes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The minor component should permeate (always favorable in membrane separation)
texts.text: The minor component should permeate (always favorable in membrane separation)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · To benefit from high selectivities, one should start from highly concentrated feeds (hence hybrid processes are favorable, e.g. distillation + membrane separation)
texts.text: To benefit from high selectivities, one should start from highly concentrated feeds (hence hybrid processes are favorable, e.g. distillation + membrane separation)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Working at elevated temperatures facilitates the process (the latent heat of vaporization has to be provided with the feed)
texts.text: Working at elevated temperatures facilitates the process (the latent heat of vaporization has to be provided with the feed)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The pressure ratio is usually increased by applying vacuum on the permeate side (e.g. by cooling condensation of the permeate stream)
texts.text: The pressure ratio is usually increased by applying vacuum on the permeate side (e.g. by cooling condensation of the permeate stream)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Mass transfer resistance through the surface boundary layer has to be considered (especially if high purity of the retentate stream is desired)
texts.text: Mass transfer resistance through the surface boundary layer has to be considered (especially if high purity of the retentate stream is desired)

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Separation Processes Laboratory - Prof. Mazzotti - Rate Controlled Separations
texts.text: Separation Processes Laboratory - Prof. Mazzotti - Rate Controlled Separations

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Separation of organic solvent mixtures in cases where distillation is difficult (Methanol / MTBE), (Benzene / Cyclohexane)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Distillation of 60% EtOH-water feed
texts.text: Distillation of 60% EtOH-water feed

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Products are the azeotrope (95.6% EtOH, top) and water (bottom)
texts.text: Products are the azeotrope (95.6% EtOH, top) and water (bottom)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Pervaporation of the azeotrope yields a retentate of > 99.5% EtOH (product)
texts.text: Pervaporation of the azeotrope yields a retentate of > 99.5% EtOH (product)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The permeate (25% EtOH) is condensed and recycled to the distillation column
texts.text: The permeate (25% EtOH) is condensed and recycled to the distillation column

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Similar setups for dehydration of other azeotrope forming organic solvents
texts.text: Similar setups for dehydration of other azeotrope forming organic solvents
